posts.content: 2025 LLM Year in Review. 2025 has been a strong and eventful year of progress in LLMs. The following is a list of personally notable and mildly surprising "paradigm changes" - things that altered the landscape and stood out to me conceptually. At the start of 2025, the LLM production stack in all labs looked something like this: ...
posts.highlights: Karpathy reviews major paradigm changes in LLMs during 2025, noting shifts in the production stack.
posts.worthReading: Provides a high-level summary of key LLM trends from a leading AI researcher.

posts.content: Judging by my tl there is a growing gap in understanding of AI capability. The first issue I think is around recency and tier of use. I think a lot of people tried the free tier of ChatGPT somewhere last year and allowed it to inform their views on AI a little too much.
posts.highlights: Karpathy observes a growing gap in AI capability understanding, noting that many base their views on outdated free-tier ChatGPT experiences.
posts.worthReading: Highlights a common misconception about AI progress and the importance of staying current.
